As long as a team’s bed remains intact, players can respawn. Teams gather resources, upgrade gear, and build bridges to attack enemies. The last team standing wins.

fields.shortDescription: This food-themed murder mystery blends cooking with crime-solving. Teams must prepare dishes while decoding culinary clues and riddles to identify a murderer. This event tests teamwork, creativity, and deduction under pressure to win the game.
fields.longDescription:
It is an immersive, food-themed murder mystery event where cooking meets crime-solving. The event is set in a fictional kitchen where a famous chef has been mysteriously “eliminated,” and the only clues left behind are hidden within recipes, ingredients, and cooking techniques. Participants are divided into teams, each representing suspects who were present during the incident.

Each team is given a mystery recipe box containing ingredients, recipe cards, and secret clues. While preparing the dish, teams must analyze food-related hints, decode culinary riddles, and piece together the sequence of events leading to the crime. Every cooking task completed correctly reveals new evidence or twists in the story.

Teams must balance speed, accuracy, and deduction, as incorrect cooking choices may lead to misleading clues. Judges act as investigators, interacting with teams and introducing surprise plot turns. The team that successfully completes the dish and correctly identifies the murderer, motive, and method wins the event.

This event encourages teamwork, creativity, time management, and critical thinking, making it a perfect blend of entertainment and challenge.

fields.shortDescription: This challenge tests prompt-design skills using large language models. Teams must complete diverse tasks using only a single, comprehensive prompt. Entries are judged on accuracy and creativity, with the highest score determining the winner.
fields.longDescription: This is a team-based challenge that tests creativity, strategy, and prompt-design skills using large language models. Teams are given a set of diverse mini-tasks, including content generation, summarization, and creative problem-solving. The key constraint is that each team may use only one single prompt to interact with an LLM of their choice, which must be designed to handle all tasks without follow-up inputs. Entries are evaluated based on accuracy, creativity, completeness, and consistency across tasks. The team with the highest overall score is declared the winner.

fields.shortDescription: In the Shadow Cipher Protocol, teams act as investigators to recover a missing Dean File. By decoding complex ciphers and symbolic clues hidden across campus, participants must outsmart The Veiled Circle before time expires to complete the mission.
fields.longDescription:
A top-secret Dean File has vanished from the college archives.
A covert group calling itself The Veiled Circle has scattered coded messages, symbolic clues, and misleading trails across the campus.
Nothing is direct.
Every clue is layered.
Every location is hidden behind riddles, shapes, and encrypted messages.

Your team must think like real investigators — decode ciphers, uncover symbolic pathways, break false clues, and uncover the truth.

Only the sharpest team will reach the final chamber, unlock the master seal, 
and retrieve the missing Dean File before time runs out.

Are you ready to enter the Shadow Cipher Protocol?

fields.shortDescription: Teams represent contingents in a multi-sport Olympic-style event featuring tech-friendly mini-challenges. Testing speed, coordination, and problem-solving, participants rotate through arenas to earn points and climb the leaderboard for victory.
fields.longDescription:
The Olympics is a team-based event designed to recreate the excitement and structure of a multi-sport Olympic Games. Teams of 3–4 participants represent a single contingent and compete across a series of distinct mini tech-friendly challenges, each functioning as an independent challenge. These events are designed to test a balanced mix of speed, coordination, problem-solving, and teamwork.
Participants rotate across multiple event arenas, earning points based on performance and strategy, with cumulative scores tracked on a central leaderboard. Timed rounds, structured progression, and surprise elements ensure a dynamic and engaging experience throughout the competition.

fields.shortDescription: In this challenge, teams recreate traditional Indian regional breakfasts from outside their own culture. Using limited resources, participants must research and cook authentic dishes. It promotes cultural learning, problem-solving, and collaboration.
fields.longDescription:
The Regional Breakfast Challenge is a unique cooking-based event designed to celebrate India’s rich culinary diversity. In this event, participating teams will be randomly assigned a traditional regional breakfast dish from India, such as Idli–Sambar, Poha, Upma, Paratha, Appam, or Dhokla. The key twist is that none of the team members will belong to the region from which the dish originates, encouraging participants to step outside their comfort zones.

Teams will be provided with basic ingredients and utensils, along with a limited preparation time. They must research, understand, and recreate the dish from scratch using only the resources provided. No prior practice or outside assistance will be allowed during the event. Participants will be judged on taste, authenticity, presentation, teamwork, and time management.

The event promotes cross-cultural learning, creativity, and collaboration, while giving students a hands-on experience of India’s culinary heritage. It also encourages adaptability and problem-solving under pressure, making it both educational and entertaining. The Regional Breakfast Challenge blends fun with learning, turning cooking into a vibrant cultural experience.

fields.shortDescription: This two-phase event blends a campus-wide RPG quest with tactical combat. Teams earn skills and gold through mini-games to prepare for a knockout, turn-based battle tournament. The last team standing after the final simulation wins.
fields.longDescription:
This two-part adventure-combat event blends strategy, teamwork, and immersive gameplay. In Level 1: Adventure Begins, teams of three navigate a quest-based storyline across campus using a provided map. At each quest location, NPC hosts guide teams through competitive mini-games and challenges. Winning quests rewards teams with levels, skill points, and gold, which can later be used to purchase skills. Special features include an Endless Mode combat zone for extra rewards and roaming Bandits who ambush teams for surprise battles. The first teams to successfully complete four quests advance.

In Level 2: Tournament Phase, qualified teams enter a knockout-style, turn-based battle tournament. Using characters developed in Level 1 (max level 6, two skills per member), teams battle via simulation software with host mediation and occasional dice rolls. The last team standing wins, making this an engaging mix of treasure hunt, RPG progression, and tactical combat.

fields.shortDescription: This detective-themed treasure hunt leads student teams across campus to solve a crime. By collecting evidence and solving puzzles at various locations, participants narrow down suspects to identify the culprit in a fun and engaging way.
fields.longDescription: This event is a detective-themed treasure hunt where students work in teams to solve a crime by chasing clues across the college campus. Participants will move through different locations, collecting evidence and solving simple puzzles that lead them to the next clue. Each piece of evidence helps teams narrow down suspects by collecting more and more clues and get closer to identifying the culprit.

fields.shortDescription: Shipwreck is a philosophy-inspired puzzle journey where teams navigate ethical dilemmas and logical clues across various activity stations. This interactive role-playing experience blends problem-solving with self-discovery to explore choice and responsib
fields.longDescription: Shipwreck is an interactive, philosophy-inspired event where participants progress through a series of activity stations, each presenting a brain, math, or philosophical puzzle. Based on their responses to ethical dilemmas and logical clues, teams move to different tables, tracing a unique path toward the finish line. The experience draws from classic moral thought experiments, encouraging reflection on choice, responsibility, and decision-making under constraints. Designed like a role-playing journey, the event combines problem-solving with self-discovery and philosophical insight in an engaging, game-based format.

fields.shortDescription: This multi-room challenge tests teams through cinematic puzzles, rapid reaction tasks, and a high-stakes logic-based boss battle. Participants must demonstrate speed, creativity, and composure to solve clues and achieve victory.
fields.longDescription: The Multi-Themed Challenge Rooms event is a fast-paced, team-based competition designed to test creativity, logic, and performance under pressure. Room 1: Movie-Based Challenge Room engages participants with cinematic puzzles such as identifying films from background scores, guessing movies through character descriptions, silent acting, recognizing directors’ signature styles, and solving film-inspired crime cases. Teams must complete all challenges to unlock progression. Room 2: Reaction Time Challenge Room shifts focus to speed and reflexes through rapid-response tasks including color recognition, light or sound-based actions, hand-eye coordination, and instant command execution, where accuracy and timing are critical. Room 3: Boss Battle – Final Logic Strike serves as the high-stakes finale. Teams analyze structured clues to identify the boss’s exact weak point and execute a single irreversible verbal strike within strict time limits. One decision determines victory or elimination, emphasizing clarity, teamwork, and composure.

fields.shortDescription: LaserTag is a high-energy, non-contact game where players use infrared guns to tag opponents in a dark arena. Emphasizing strategy and quick reflexes, teams must score points while navigating obstacles in this immersive experience.
fields.longDescription: LaserTag is a fast-paced, non-contact game where players use infrared laser guns to tag opponents wearing sensor vests. Played in a dark, obstacle-filled arena, the game focuses on teamwork, strategy, and quick reflexes. Players score points by tagging others while avoiding being hit themselves. Safe and exciting, LaserTag is popular for parties, entertainment centers, and team-building activities, offering a fun and immersive competitive experience.

fields.shortDescription: CODE RED is a medical escape room challenge where teams navigate an emergency scenario. Participants solve clues related to first aid and disaster preparedness, making quick decisions to save lives while racing against the clock.
fields.longDescription: CODE RED is an exciting escape room–style event by the Youth Red Cross where teams step into a simulated emergency scenario and race against time to save the day. You’ll crack clues, make quick decisions, and work together to complete challenges related to first aid, health awareness, and disaster preparedness.

fields.shortDescription: The Lavani Workshop introduces participants to Maharashtra’s vibrant folk dance. Led by experts, the session covers traditional footwork, gestures, and expressions, helping beginners build confidence and connect with Marathi culture.
fields.longDescription: The Lavani Workshop is an immersive cultural experience that introduces participants to the vibrant and expressive folk dance form of Maharashtra. Known for its powerful rhythm, graceful movements, and emotive storytelling, Lavani blends music, expression, and energy into a captivating performance art. Guided by experienced performers, the workshop covers basic footwork, hand gestures, facial expressions, and an understanding of traditional costumes and music. Designed for beginners and enthusiasts alike, the session encourages participants to connect with Marathi culture while building confidence, coordination, and stage presence. The workshop celebrates the spirit, elegance, and timeless charm of Lavani.

fields.shortDescription: This Socratic-inspired philosophy debate challenges participants to examine ethical concepts through logical reasoning. Featuring turncoat rounds and structured discussions, it focuses on critical thinking, questioning assumptions, and coherent analysis.
fields.longDescription: This event is a philosophy-based debate competition inspired by the Socratic method. Participants engage in structured discussions that encourage questioning assumptions, defending ethical viewpoints, and examining concepts such as justice, democracy, and authority. The format includes turncoat rounds, where speakers switch positions, and short ice-breaker activities to promote critical thinking and engagement. Emphasis is placed on logical reasoning, clarity of argument, and thoughtful analysis rather than consensus, with judgments based on the strength and coherence of reasoning presented.

fields.shortDescription: This premier gaming festival celebrates digital innovation and competitive spirit through esports tournaments, console gaming, and virtual reality. It provides a platform for participants to showcase strategic thinking, skill, and a passion for excellence
fields.longDescription: This gaming fest is a premier event celebrating the spirit of digital competition and innovation. It features engaging esports tournaments, console gaming, and virtual reality experiences. Designed to challenge skills and foster camaraderie, the fest offers participants an opportunity to showcase talent, strategic thinking, and passion for gaming excellence.

fields.shortDescription: This individual basketball challenge tests precision and creativity as participants shoot from various angles and positions. For a small entry fee, players can showcase their accuracy in this engaging edition focused on skillful, high-difficulty hoop shot
fields.longDescription: DudePerfect Basketball Edition – An exciting individual challenge where participants must shoot the basketball into the hoop from different angles and positions. The registration fee is ₹50, and it’s strictly an individual event, testing precision and creativity in every throw.

fields.shortDescription: Hosted by the Women Development Cell, Ground Zero is a knockout tournament blending rapid-fire quizzes, SDG challenges, and intense escape room scenarios. It promotes gender equality and empowerment while testing participants’ problem-solving and logic sk
fields.longDescription:
Ground Zero erupts as the ultimate battleground for brains, bravado, and breakneck escapes! Hosted by the Women Development Cell, this pulse-pounding knockout tournament unleashes solo showdowns and duo duels fueled by rapid-fire general knowledge clashes, Sustainable Development Goals (SDGs) brain-benders, and heart-racing escape room scenarios.

Picture this: time-locked chambers where every second counts—crack cryptic puzzles, outsmart high-stakes riddles, and blast through immersive traps under crushing countdowns, all while forging unbreakable bonds or unleashing solo fury to survive eliminations.

Beyond the adrenaline overload, Ground Zero sparks fierce conversations on gender equality, equal opportunities, and collective empowerment, transforming rivals into revolutionaries for a bolder world.

fields.shortDescription: BoxCricket is a fast-paced tournament played in a compact arena, featuring teams of five players and three substitutes. This accessible competition emphasizes strategy and agility, offering an exciting experience for all cricket enthusiasts for a modest f
fields.longDescription: BoxCricket – A fast-paced cricket event played in a compact arena. Each team consists of 5 main players and 3 substitutes, ensuring flexibility and strategy. The registration fee is ₹199, making it an accessible and exciting competition for all cricket enthusiasts.

fields.shortDescription: The Hotseat is a high-energy multi-stage competition featuring rapid-fire quizzes, survey-style rounds, and a KBC-inspired finale. Teams of two test their quick thinking, strategy, and debating skills in this hybrid quiz event designed for engagement.
fields.longDescription:
The Hotseat is a dynamic flagship event that tests knowledge, wit, and debating skills in a high-energy, multi-stage competition. The event kicks off with the Speed Zone, where teams of two tackle a rapid-fire pen-and-paper round, answering as many questions as possible within a set time. This round challenges participants’ quick thinking and accuracy, setting the tone for the competition. The top teams then move on to the Family Feud, a lively and interactive round where teams guess the most popular answers to survey-style questions, earning points based on audience-approved responses. This round emphasizes teamwork, strategy, and quick thinking, while keeping the energy fun and engaging.

The intensity escalates as the final five teams earn the chance to compete in a live KBC-style round, featuring buzzer-based questions, audience participation, and strategic gameplay. It is a hybrid quiz-debate event that combines suspense, knowledge and creativity.

fields.shortDescription: This inter-college parliamentary debate offers a structured platform for intellectual discourse on global issues. Teams of three use critical thinking and persuasive speaking to argue positions, fostering democratic values, confidence, and socio-political
fields.longDescription: This Inter-College Parliamentary Debate Competition aims to provide a platform for students to engage in structured, intellectual discourse on contemporary national and global issues. The event follows the parliamentary style of debating, encouraging critical thinking, persuasive speaking, teamwork, and quick rebuttals. Each team consists of three speakers who represent either the Proposition or the Opposition, debating a motion released shortly before the rounds. The competition fosters democratic values, public speaking confidence, and awareness of socio-political matters among students.The final round will determine the winning team, with attractive prizes awarded to the best teams and speakers.

fields.shortDescription: This high-intensity, Punjab-themed escape room challenges teams to crack handcrafted puzzles and solve logic problems within fifteen minutes. Participants must work quickly to unlock symbolic mechanisms and collect ten keys to escape this structured story
fields.longDescription:
A high-intensity escape room built entirely from scratch, inspired by Punjab’s rich structure, symbols, and problem-solving traditions.
Teams have 15 minutes to crack handcrafted puzzles, solve logic challenges, and unlock mechanisms to collect 10 symbolic cards/keys woven into a Punjab-structured storyline.
Only teams that think fast, work smart, and adapt quickly will make it out.

fields.eventName: PLAYVERSE: The Ultimate Campus Game Aren
fields.shortDescription: PLAYVERSE is a dynamic campus gaming event featuring a variety of custom mini-games and easy-to-play classics. Teams rotate through stations, earning points for performance. It emphasizes teamwork, quick thinking, and inclusive fun for all students.
fields.longDescription:
PLAYVERSE is a large-scale campus gaming event where students compete in teams by playing a variety of simple, fun, and engaging games.
The event features a mix of custom-built mini games developed by OSPC, along with classic and easy-to-play games that require no prior experience or gaming skills.

Each team rotates through multiple game stations, earning points based on performance.
At the end of all rounds, the team with the highest total score is declared the winner.

The event focuses on fun, teamwork, quick thinking, and engagement, making it accessible to students from all departments and years.

fields.shortDescription: MADHOUSE is a high-energy team event featuring unpredictable, surprise challenges that test coordination and creativity. Participants engage in physical and communication-based tasks, earning points through teamwork and enthusiasm in a fun atmosphere.
fields.longDescription: MADHOUSE is a high-energy, fun-filled team event designed to test coordination, creativity, and adaptability through unpredictable challenges. Teams face a series of surprise tasks revealed on the spot, ranging from physical coordination games and blindfold challenges to quick-thinking and communication-based activities. No prior skills or preparation are required—success depends purely on teamwork, presence of mind, and enthusiasm. Each round earns teams points based on task completion, creativity, and overall performance. With constant twists and humorous challenges, MADHOUSE ensures continuous engagement for both participants and the audience, making it an exciting and inclusive campus event.

fields.shortDescription: Secret Agenda is an interactive game of strategy and deception for teams of two. Participants use logic, storytelling, and props to uncover truths or hide information. The audience actively participates by spotting lies and voting on successful deceptions
fields.longDescription:
Secret Agenda is a round-wise, interactive game designed around deception, strategy, and communication. Played in teams of two, the event challenges participants to speak convincingly, hide information, and uncover the truth using logic, storytelling, and physical props.

Across multiple rounds, teams face unique challenges: defending sealed statements using props, identifying lies through questioning across a barrier, solving questions with limited clues, and spotting false evidence presented as truth. In every round, one player knows more than the other, creating tension and mind-games that test trust, observation, and persuasion.

The audience plays an active role throughout by guessing outcomes, spotting inconsistencies, and voting on successful deception, making them insiders rather than passive viewers. With a balance of thinking and performance, Secret Agenda offers intelligent, engaging fun without cringe.

fields.shortDescription: Midnight Protocol is a narrative-driven investigation where teams analyze evidence, decode clues, and make strategic decisions. This immersive thriller focuses on accuracy and critical thinking across structured phases, challenging participants to solve a
fields.longDescription: Midnight Protocol is a narrative-driven investigation event conducted in structured phases. Participants register as teams and are briefed on a fictional crisis involving leaked information, missing data, and conflicting narratives. Teams receive evidence folders containing documents, transcripts, codes, and misleading leads. Over the next hour, they must analyze information, decode clues, question moderators acting as in-game characters, and make written decisions at fixed checkpoints. Each decision alters the difficulty and direction of subsequent tasks. The event is not speed-based; accuracy and reasoning matter more than rushing. Teams document their conclusions and submit final reports explaining their logic. The experience feels like being inside a live thriller, requiring sustained focus, collaboration, and critical thinking throughout.

fields.shortDescription: Thanima presents a premium cultural showcase celebrating diversity through art, music, and dance. This event provides a platform for emerging talent to exhibit their creativity and fosters campus unity by blending traditional heritage with modern expressi
fields.longDescription: Thanima proudly presents a premium cultural event designed to celebrate the vibrant diversity of traditions, art forms, and creative expressions present across the campus. This event serves as a dynamic platform that brings together students from varied cultural backgrounds, fostering unity through art, music, dance, fashion, and performance. Beyond celebration, the event is envisioned as an opportunity for upcoming artists to step into the spotlight and confidently exhibit their talent before a wider audience. By encouraging originality, innovation, and cultural pride, Thanima aims to nurture emerging performers and creators, helping them gain visibility and recognition. The event reflects the spirit of inclusivity and artistic excellence, creating an atmosphere where tradition meets modern expression. Through carefully curated performances and experiences, the showcase highlights the richness of cultural heritage while inspiring collaboration and mutual respect among participants. Ultimately, this premium event stands as a testament to Thanima’s commitment to empowering talent, preserving culture, and strengthening the cultural fabric of the campus.

fields.shortDescription: Magadh Mahotsav Carnival at Vibrance’26 is a lively cultural celebration hosted by the Magadh Mithila Club. Duos can participate in fun, interactive games and enjoy a buzzing atmosphere filled with festive energy for a nominal entry fee per pair.
fields.longDescription:
🎡 Magadh Mahotsav Carnival | Vibrance’26 🎶

Magadh Mithila Club presents to you the Magadh Mahotsav Carnival, a lively fusion of joy, culture, and celebration at Vibrance’26. Step into a buzzing carnival space where every corner promises excitement, packed with fun and interactive games that challenge, entertain, and bring out your playful side. Jump in, take on the challenges, and let the carnival energy carry you through.

✨ Entry: Duos only
💰 Fee: ₹50 per duo

The stalls are set, the games are calling, let the fun begin! ✨

fields.shortDescription: Mission Apollo is an immersive space-themed escape room challenging teams to solve puzzles and restore vital systems under pressure. Participants must decode signals and navigate mazes to ensure a safe return to Earth, highlighting collaboration and logic
fields.longDescription: Mission Apollo: The Last Exit is an immersive, space-themed room escape where participants will test their intelligence, teamwork, and problem-solving skills to operate under pressure. On a critical Apollo mission, the crew faced a sudden system failure, leaving them locked inside the spacecraft. With time running out, teams must work together to decode signals, navigate intricate mazes, solve space-inspired puzzles, and restore vital systems to ensure their safe return to Earth. The escape room is specially designed to simulate a real space mission environment filled with cryptic clues, emergency protocols, and mission-critical challenges that require logical reasoning and quick decision-making. The event promotes collaboration, analytical thinking, and good communication skills with an engaging mix of learning and entertainment. Mission Apollo: The Last Exit is an experience that promises you more than you bargained for; it is hands-on, with every second counting, and only the sharpest teams will make it out on time.

fields.shortDescription: This team-based challenge involves completing movie-themed tasks across various booths using quest cards. Duos earn points based on task difficulty and hidden wildcard challenges. The team with the highest score within forty-five minutes is crowned the wi
fields.longDescription:
Team size - 2
Duration - 45 to 60 mins

A team-based challenge where players complete movie-themed tasks across hidden booths. Each completed task earns points based on difficulty. Quest cards guide teams through the game, with wildcard challenges offering bonus points. The team with the highest score in 45 minutes wins.


1. Each booth offers tasks of difficulty, Easy (1 point) - Medium (3 points) - Hard (5 points) 
2. Completion of each task is to be reported to the Taskmaster booth.
3. After each task, teams receive a Quest Card leading to the next booth. Teams can only do one task per booth.
4. Wildcard challenges are hidden across the venue; completing them earns bonus points.
5. The team with the highest total score at the end of 45 minutes wins. In case of a tie, a sudden death task decides the winner.

For instance, consider a Marvel themed Booth... So, the Quest card would say something like:
"Multiple timelines are overlapping, and key events are out of place. The TVA has gone silent. You must intervene.”
In the booth the challenges would be smt like: 
Easy: Do a superhero pose and name 5 superheroes in 10 seconds.
Medium: Match 5 quotes to their Marvel characters.
Hard: Enact a short superhero fight scene using slow motion.

fields.shortDescription: This vibrant cosplay event invites participants to dress as assigned characters and showcase their skills through spontaneous acting and a ramp walk. It combines costume design with performance, challenging attendees to embody roles from specific movies.
fields.longDescription: A cosplay event - participants will be allotted certain characters and they have to dress up as the character and on the spot any scene would be given and they have to act accordingly as per the movie character along with a ramp walk

fields.shortDescription: This relaxing hangout features an air-dry clay activity, live music requests, and a photo booth for students. Participants can enjoy thumbprint Polaroids and refreshments, creating a fun, creative atmosphere for everyone to socialize and unwind.
fields.longDescription: The event will have an air-dry clay activity as the main attraction, along with live music based on students’ requests. There will also be a photo booth, a thumbprint Polaroid activity, and pani puri served as refreshment, making it a fun and enjoyable hangout for everyone.

fields.shortDescription: Two teams compete by singing songs containing specific words announced by a moderator. Using green and red cards, teams claim points or challenge opponents. This musical contest features knockout rounds leading to a final showdown with vocal performances.
fields.longDescription:
At the center sits a POPC member with a timer and a displayed word. Two teams of three players each face one another from opposite tables, ready to react. Each team receives a green card and a red card.

When a word is announced, a team that knows a song containing that word raises the green card and must sing the lyric aloud. A correct song earns +1 point. If the opposing team believes the answer is incorrect, they may challenge using the red card. If the challenge is valid, the challengers gain +2 points. If incorrect, they lose 1 point.

Matches progress through knockout rounds including semifinals and finals. Regular rounds play to 5 or 7 points, while the final goes up to 10 points. Singing is mandatory. A POPC member maintains the live score, and microphones are provided for clear performance.

fields.eventName: Art for Change -  Painting & Drawing Competition
fields.shortDescription: Art for Change is a platform where participants use painting and drawing to address social issues like health and climate change. Artworks are judged on creativity and impact, encouraging critical thinking while nurturing artistic talent and awareness.
fields.longDescription: Art for Change is a creative platform that encourages participants to express their ideas and concerns about pressing social issues through painting and drawing. This edition focuses on themes of health awareness and climate change, allowing students to visually communicate messages that inspire reflection and action. Participants can use any medium, style, or technique to bring their concepts to life on paper or canvas. The artworks will be judged on creativity, originality, clarity of message, visual appeal, and relevance to the chosen theme. This event not only nurtures artistic talent but also raises awareness about important societal issues among participants and viewers alike. By combining art with meaningful social commentary, Art for Change aims to make participants think critically while providing an enjoyable and expressive experience.

fields.shortDescription: Battle of Culture is a performance competition where teams showcase art forms like dance, music, or drama representing their heritage. Judged by experts and audience response, the event celebrates diverse cultures and teamwork through a high-energy stage
fields.longDescription: Battle of Culture is a friendly performance face-off where creativity meets fun. The event kicks off with a short introduction of the participants and a quick audience warm-up to set the mood. Each team or performer then takes the stage to present their act—this could be dance, music, drama, or any art form that represents their cultural roots or personal style.After every performance, the judges share brief comments, and the audience gets a chance to cheer for their favorites. Once all acts have performed, there’s a short break before the final results. The judges’ scores and crowd response decide who takes home the prize money.The evening wraps up with a light-hearted closing segment, celebrating all participants and the diverse cultures they bring together. It’s an event about expression, teamwork, and enjoying art in its many forms.

fields.shortDescription: In this unique event, blindfolded participants must guess and report on movie clips playing behind them. By interacting with judges to catch subtle hints, they describe the action, testing their intuition and improvisational skills in a creative way.
fields.longDescription:
Green screen 
In this event, participants will be blind folded in front of a screen on which a random famous movie clip will be played. 
The participants are not aware of what clip is playing behind them however they must act and report on what's going on behind them while maintaining conversation with the judges where the judge will drop subtle hints about the clip for the participants to catch on

fields.shortDescription: GameShift is a high-energy team event where simple games evolve through sudden rule disruptions. Teams must adapt strategies to changing dynamics. The challenge emphasizes flexibility, creativity, and teamwork, providing a fast-paced experience for partic
fields.longDescription:
GameShift is a fast-paced, interactive team-based event where participants play simple games that constantly evolve through unexpected rule changes. Teams are first briefed on a base game and its workflow. These base games can include activities like throwing a ball at a target, memory or pattern challenges, coordination-based tasks, or logic and strategy mini-games.

As the game progresses, periodic “disruptions” are introduced every few minutes, altering the rules, team dynamics, time limits, or communication methods. These disruptions force teams to quickly adapt, rethink strategies, and coordinate under pressure.

The focus of GameShift is not just winning, but demonstrating adaptability, creativity, and teamwork in a constantly changing environment. The event is designed to be fun, engaging, and accessible to all students, with minimal resources and maximum participation. By the end of the event, teams will have experienced multiple shifts in gameplay, making it a high-energy and memorable experience for both participants and spectators.

fields.shortDescription: This 45-minute strategy game pits crew members against hidden killers. Crew wins by completing tasks or voting out killers, while killers eliminate players. Featuring roles like Doctor and Policeman, the game emphasizes deduction through meetings and voti
fields.longDescription:
Objective:
Crew wins by completing all tasks or voting out both killers.
Killers win by eliminating enough players to stop tasks.

Tasks:
5 simple tasks (puzzles, riddles, clue finding). 2–3 players per task. Safe areas only.

Kills:
Killers show a Kill Card. No kills in task zones, washrooms, or crowded areas.

Powers:
Doctor – revive 1 player (once, within 10 min).
Policeman – check 2 players (during meetings).
Captain – cancel 1 wrong vote.

Meetings:
Max 2, 3 min discussion, compulsory voting.

Time: 45 minutes.
Rules: No rock climbing, running, or physical contact.

fields.shortDescription: This event is a collaboration between TEDx VIT Chennai, History TV18, City of Kitchener, and the United Nations. It features a series of insightful talks designed to explore the significant ideas and forces that shape our modern world today.
fields.longDescription: A collaborative event between TEDX VIT CHENNAI, History TV18, City of Kitchener and United Nations where we will be having a series  of talks that shape the modern world.

fields.shortDescription: Emergency Exit 3.0 is a team-based escape room challenge set in a classroom. Participants solve a sequence of puzzles and clues within a strict time limit to find the final key. The event focuses on logical reasoning, effort, and quick problem-solving ski
fields.longDescription: Emergency Exit 3.0 is a team-based escape room event conducted within a closed classroom environment. Participants compete in small teams, with multiple teams playing at the same time. The room contains a sequence of clues and puzzles that must be identified and solved to progress. Teams work within a fixed time limit to locate the final key or solution required to exit the room. The event follows a structured rule set and is designed around problem-solving, logical reasoning, and coordinated team effort.

fields.shortDescription: InFYInite is a signature FYI event designed to inform, involve, and inspire students through creative challenges. It focuses on unconventional thinking and celebrates originality by blending problem-solving with teamwork to showcase innovative ideas.
fields.longDescription: InFYInite is one of FYI’s signature events designed to Inform, Involve, and Inspire students through a dynamic blend of creativity, problem-solving, and teamwork. InFYInite focuses on unconventional thinking and celebrates originality.

fields.shortDescription: Monopoly Rush blends campus mini-games with strategic gameplay. Participants earn currency to enter Monopoly rounds and invest for leaderboard points. Success requires careful planning, as only game points determine the final winners, making every decisio
fields.longDescription:
Participants begin by exploring different zones on campus, competing in engaging mini-games to earn in-game currency based on their performance. This earned money becomes the key to unlocking Monopoly rounds, where players must make smart financial decisions—choosing how much to invest and when to take risks.

Each monopoly round rewards points depending on gameplay and strategy, and only these points contribute to the final leaderboard. Any unused cash or mini-game earnings don’t carry over, making every decision count. Once spent, money is gone—so careful planning and bold moves are essential.

The objective is simple yet challenging: climb the leaderboard by maximizing points through Monopoly rounds. With a perfect mix of skill, strategy, and luck, Monopoly Rush promises an unforgettable, high-energy competitive experience.

fields.shortDescription: Dare Dashers is a team-based board game where participants navigate a numbered path by rolling dice. Landing on specific squares requires completing various dares to progress. The event emphasizes fun and ends when a team reaches the final square on the b
fields.longDescription: Dare Dashers is a team-based interactive event centered around a dice-driven board game format. Participants advance along a numbered path by rolling a large dice and moving forward according to the value obtained. The game path consists of multiple squares, some of which are mapped to specific categories of dares. When a team lands on such a square, they are required to complete the assigned task as per the rules of that category before continuing. The tasks vary in nature and are designed to maintain active participation throughout the event. The game proceeds until a team reaches the final square on the board, which marks the completion of the event.

fields.shortDescription: A story-driven multiverse mystery where Sherlock’s death echoes across realities. Teams traverse collapsing universes, solve logic, tech, audio, and illusion puzzles, eliminate false timelines, and use limited time travel to uncover the one true crime.
fields.longDescription:
Sherlock exists across multiple parallel realities. When the multiverse fractures, he is found dead in every universe and each death an echo of a single, true crime. Only one universe holds the real murder. The rest are distortions created by collapsing timelines.

Teams must travel across these universes, each governed by different rules, challenges, and truths. As realities overlap and time itself destabilizes, participants gather fragmented clues about the killer, the weapon, and the world by eliminating false paths to uncover the one reality where the crime truly occurred.

Challenges include logic and tech puzzles, audio-based decoding, visual illusion tasks, hands-on team activities, and chaos relay featuring elements like Morse-code walk, riddle resolutions,sabotage cards, etc.

A limited time-travel window allows teams to revisit the past, alter their deductions, and gain critical insights before the multiverse collapses completely.

A high-intensity, story-driven mystery that blends multiverse chaos, strategic deduction, and immersive gameplay.

fields.shortDescription: An interactive event where participants channel-surf through movie genres, catch rapid snippets, and read the crowd to decide which film to continue—testing intuition, adaptability, and audience engagement in real time.
fields.longDescription: Imagine an event where you flip through movie channels, catching snippets of different films, and pausing on the ones that catch your eye 👀. You could have a lineup of genres - action, rom-com, thriller, horror - and participants must act on which movie the audiences want to stay tuned for.

fields.shortDescription: A fast-paced circuit-based escape room where teams solve semiconductor puzzles, logic clues, and electronics riddles under time pressure, testing creativity, teamwork, and real-world engineering problem-solving skills.
fields.longDescription: Escape Room: Engineer’s Gambit is a Circuital Based paced, challenge where participants solve puzzles on semiconductor based break outs before time runs out. The room is filled with logic clues, technical riddles on electronics, and real-world problem-solving tasks that test creativity, teamwork, and quick thinking. Every decision matters, and collaboration is key to cracking the final escape. It’s an exciting, hands-on experience that brings out the engineer’s instinct to analyze, adapt, and innovate under pressure.
